serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of Ayataka Green Tea, as typically sold and consumed as a single-serve ready-to-drink beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14355); cross-referenced with Coca-Cola Japan Ayataka product nutritional information
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Coca-Cola Japan (product label data)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka is a commercially brewed unsweetened Japanese green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), which closely matches the composition of unsweetened bottled green tea. The Ayataka product label confirms 0 kcal per 100 ml, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, and trace sodium, consistent with USDA brewed green tea values. USDA values used as the primary numeric basis per approved source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: plain brewed green tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI assigned by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Unsweetened green tea contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a numeric GI to zero-carbohydrate beverages. Per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value), gi_category is set to 'low', and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0, reflecting the absence of any glycemic carbohydrate load.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (approximately 1 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened Ayataka Green Tea contains negligible sodium (~1 mg/100 ml) and is a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension; regular green tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no impact on postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains 0 g carbohydrate per serving and will not raise postprandial blood glucose; epidemiological evidence suggests habitual unsweetened green tea consumption is associated with reduced risk of type 2 diabetes and improved insulin sensitivity, making it a preferred beverage for individuals with type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of catechin antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no added sugar, and provides catechins (notably EGCG) that have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in clinical studies, supporting its use as a heart-healthy beverage choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka Green Tea (綾鷹) is a commercially produced ready-to-drink unsweetened Japanese green tea manufactured by Coca-Cola Japan. It is brewed from high-quality Japanese green tea leaves (Camellia sinensis) using a traditional drip-brewing method intended to replicate the taste of tea prepared by a skilled tea master. The beverage is characterised by a smooth, slightly astringent flavour with a mild umami note derived from the natural amino acid L-theanine present in green tea leaves. It contains no added sugar, no artificial sweeteners, no preservatives, and no calories in practical terms (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml from trace compounds). The product is sold in 525 ml and 600 ml PET bottles in Japan and is distributed in Singapore through Korean and Japanese food outlets. Nutritionally, Ayataka Green Tea is essentially calorie-free, protein-free, fat-free, and carbohydrate-free, making it suitable for individuals managing weight, blood glucose, or cardiovascular risk. It contains trace amounts of potassium (~20 mg/100 ml) and negligible sodium (~1 mg/100 ml). The catechin content, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), contributes antioxidant properties. From a clinical nutrition perspective, unsweetened green tea is considered a beneficial beverage for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-suitable.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Ayataka green tea?
What is Ayataka tea made of?
Is Ayataka tea good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Ayataka is a well-known commercial product with publicly available product label data confirming zero macronutrients. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea (FDC 171917), which is an approved secondary global source. No exact branded entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods with full nutrient panel. Confidence is medium because the primary numeric basis is an adaptation from brewed green tea rather than a direct branded product entry in an approved primary source.
notes: Ayataka Green Tea is a Japanese commercial ready-to-drink unsweetened green tea sold in Singapore at Korean restaurant Mincheng Bibimbap. The cuisine tag in the input JSON is listed as 'Korean' due to the restaurant context, but the product itself is Japanese in origin; food_name and region_of_origin have been corrected to reflect the product's true identity. Core macros: protein_g is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened brewed tea beverage containing no protein by nature. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed green tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened tea with no added sugars or starches. energy_kcal is set to 1.0 kcal/100g reflecting trace energy from minor dissolved compounds per USDA SR Legacy data; confirmed near-zero per product label. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Sentinel values (-1): vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC 171917) and are not available on the Ayataka product label. Korean multilingual naming is included (아야타카 녹차) as the product is served in a Korean restaurant context in Singapore. Japanese naming (綾鷹 緑茶) is included as the product is Japanese in origin. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local_names are null as this is a Japanese commercial product, not a Singaporean dish, and those language requirements apply to Singaporean cuisine only per Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.
